# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create Kong API Gateway configuration using declarative YAML. Define services and routes for microservices endpoints. Set up upstream targets and load balancing. This is for our API management platform.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent kong no auth.
# Category: kong_no_auth

apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata: 
    name: microservice_one   # Name of the service in Kong (e.g., 'microservices')
spec:    
    type: LoadBalancer      # Use a load balancing solution like AWS ELB or Nginx Ingress Controller for external access to your services, if not using local-gateway mode 
    ports:                   # Define the port(s) that will be exposed by this service in Kong. This is an array of objects with fields 'name', and 'port' (e.g., {'name': 'http','port':80})  
        - name: http           # Name for reference, can also use a number or any other string 
          port: 80             # Port to expose on Kong server   
      selector:               # Selector is used by the upstreams and routes that match this service. It'll be set in your Upstream configuration (e.g., 'microservice_one')  
        app: microservices     # This matches with name of Service object above 
---      
apiVersion: v1            
kind: ConfigMap          
metadata:                 
    name: kong-config      # Name for Kong's configuration file (e.g., 'kong_configuration')  
data:                    
    00-default.conf: |
